title: Avalyn, in pursuit of better lung drugs, banks $300M in an IPO
summary: The biotech will use the proceeds to develop inhalable versions of therapies already on the market for the “idiopathic” and “progressive” forms of pulmonary fibrosis.

title: HHS appeals ruling that stalled many of Kennedy’s vaccine policy changes
summary: The appeal is the latest twist in the closely watched lawsuit filed by the American Association of Pediatrics.

title: STAT+: Supreme Court justices appear to signal ‘skinny labeling’ can survive
summary: At Wednesday's argument, the justices did not appear to question whether skinny labeling is valid.

title: STAT+: Why conversations around health AI may be evolving beyond hype
summary: In this edition of AI Prognosis, Brittany Trang argues that the tide may be turning when it comes to how the health care industry talks about AI.

title: Teva nabs experimental Tourette drug in $700M Emalex buyout
summary: A Jefferies analyst argued the deal — Teva’s largest in a decade — “makes sense” given the synergies between Emalex and the pharmaceutical giant’s neurology business.

title: Chiesi to buy KalVista in $1.9B deal for rare disease drug
summary: The acquisition is the fifth of a biotech company so far this week and, according to some Wall Street analysts, proves pharma’s interest in new and emerging oral therapies for hereditary angioedema.

title: Infant formula largely safe from heavy metals, FDA finds
summary: FDA sampled 312 major infant formula brands and found low or undetectable levels of pesticide residue, PFAS, heavy metals and phthalates.

summary: A change to FDA clinical trial data review, Rocket sells its priority review voucher, and more biotech news from The Readout

summary: The FDA announced efforts to make clinical trials more efficient, starting by reviewing data in real time from trials conducted by AstraZeneca and Amgen

title: FDA Releases Results from Largest-Ever Testing of Infant Formula in the U.S.
summary: FDA announces results from the largest and most rigorous examination ever conducted focused on chemical contaminants in infant formula available on the U.S. market.

title: Food safety experts warn of USDA brain drain
summary: An autism advisory committee finally meets again, antipsychotics and dementia, and more health news from Morning Rounds

title: Opinion: STAT+: Did Kennedy just stack the deck on FDA oversight of peptides?
summary: RFK Jr. had long signaled that he was going to do something big on peptides. Now the moment has arrived.

title: Opinion: Why alternative medicine can feel so much better than mainstream health care
summary: Modern medicine is about making people better — not necessarily making them feel good.

title: New report shows some health equity wins. Experts fear they will be short-lived
summary: A report released Wednesday highlights successes in reducing health disparities in U.S. states between 2022 and 2024.

summary: The committee met for the first time since Kennedy fired most of the committee’s scientific experts and replaced them with activists and advocates.

title: STAT+: Health system CEOs get off easy at Congressional hearing on affordability
summary: Committee members largely blamed the other party’s health care policies for driving U.S. health care prices to levels inaccessible to many Americans.

summary: The AIDS advocacy group PrEP4All has sued the Trump administration over an undisclosed agreement with Gilead.

title: FDA Announces Major Steps to Implement Real-Time Clinical Trials
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today announced two major steps as part of an initiative to advance the implementation of real-time clinical trials (RTCT).

summary: “For the first time, FDA regulators will be able to see what’s happening in a clinical trial, looking at endpoints in the cloud as they occur,” writes FDA commissioner.

title: Fauci adviser David Morens indicted on charges of concealing emails, avoiding records requests
summary: The charges against Morens come after Congressional probes related to the origins of the virus that causes Covid-19.

title: Pfizer deals extend patent life for a top-selling rare disease drug
summary: Settlements delaying the arrival of generic versions of tafamidis will protect billions in Pfizer revenue and have implications for others, including BridgeBio Pharma and Alnylam.

title: Boehringer dual-acting obesity shot hits mark in Phase 3 trial
summary: Survodutide spurred weight loss Wall Street analysts referred to as “Wegovy-like,” and showed signs it might help preserve muscle mass as well.

title: STAT+: Supreme Court to hear case about ‘skinny labeling’ and generic access
summary: The Supreme Court Wednesday will hear oral arguments about a simmering controversy over so-called skinny labels, with implications for the availability of generics.

title: FDA claims Amgen drug data were ‘manipulated’; Erasca slides despite ‘home run’ results

summary: Eli Lilly's deal with Profluent, Erasca's RAS-targeting cancer pill, and more biotech news today

title: Coultreon banks $125M to support testing of former Galapagos immune drug
summary: Backed by Novo Holdings, Regeneron’s venture arm and several others, the biotech believes an oral drug licensed from Galapagos might hold potential treating many immunological diseases.

title: Biotech investors gamble on M&A. Some drugmakers are betting on themselves instead.
summary: Young biotechs usually get acquired instead of profitably selling their own medicines. A growing number are proving they can, which could make the sector more broadly appealing to investors, some say.
